title: The Island’s Old Wounds Return
description: On pages 8–10, this part follows Usopp, Unknown person #7, Unknown person #8, Rocks D. Xebec, Unknown person #9, King Harald as The story turns toward Elbaf’s past, Loki, Rocks, Harald, and the ancient grudges shaping the island’s present conflict.
scene: The story turns toward Elbaf’s past, Loki, Rocks, Harald, and the ancient grudges shaping the island’s present conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: How do I get to Elbaf?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: The new world is that way!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Voha ha, if you survive this, let's meet again Harald!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't be ridiculous! As if I'd ever associate with a criminal like you!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What the?! Are you seriously just gonna jump off the edge?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Rocks had taken down a Navy Admiral just moments earlier.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Despite all the efforts to save him, the Admiral did not survive.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was deemed the most grievous of sins.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: in the blink of an eye, rocks' infamy boomed.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The event sent a powerful shock throughout the seas.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Some time later...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: JGWAGH!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A ship carrying a great amount of celestial tribute to Mary Geoise was attacked.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The gates of justice were destroyed, and the attacker fled the scene.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The stolen funds were later used to support rocks' pirate activities.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Until that point, rocks had always acted against the world government...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But after that, he started to target pirates too.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In the world of pirates, there exists a long-standing tradition...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: known as a day back fight.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: pirate crews would compete in a series of matches...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wagering their flags or even their crewmates in a truly ruthless game.

dialogue.speaker: Rocks D. Xebec
dialogue.text: Rocks used this tradition...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: ...to build his entire crew.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They had won many scoundrels to their side before they even had a flag.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They've got an amazing guy!! You'll lose it when you see him.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: After that...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: They made their way to Elbaf.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oh no... I rushed home as fast as I could...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But I'm glad you've already turned the corner on your recovery.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Thank goodness.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I know that you're strong, lol,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But you shouldn't wander into the realm of the dead.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You gotta join my crew!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The world government won't hear ya out right?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This is a real feast! JIHA ha ha!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You got lucky escaping with nothing more than that after falling off that cliff.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's plenty more dangers just lurking deeper inside.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You get it now?! The time has come!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Dad really came back?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It has to be a coincidence... He couldn't have come for me!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't be stupid!! If I anchor myself with a thug like you, I'll lose all the good will I've built up with my allies.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: ROGks!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What of it?! Aren't those Allies government Paws?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I've heard all about your crimes. What are you doing here?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: He sure is big!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So that's the King of Elbaf?!

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: I missed ya, Harald!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't be a wet blanket, Harald!! Elbaf has the greatest warriors in the world!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If something churns your stomach, you should just crush it!!

summary: This part advances the Elbaf’s Buried History through 49 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
